Class UserFileRepository

java.lang.Object
org.example.repository.InMemoryRepository<Long,User>
org.example.repository.AbstractFileRepository<Long,User>
org.example.repository.UserFileRepository
All Implemented Interfaces:
Repository<Long,User>
public class UserFileRepository extends AbstractFileRepository<Long,User>
Repository for storing User entities in a file. Supports both Person and Duck types. Extends AbstractFileRepository and implements conversion between file lines and entity objects.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    UserFileRepository(String fileName)
    Constructs a new UserFileRepository with the given file name.

    Converts a User entity into a string suitable for storing in a file.

    Converts a list of strings (read from a file line) into a User entity.

  • Constructor Details

    • UserFileRepository

      public UserFileRepository(String fileName)
      Constructs a new UserFileRepository with the given file name.
      Parameters:
      fileName - the name of the file where users are stored

  • Method Details

    • extractEntity

      public User extractEntity(List<String> data)
      Converts a list of strings (read from a file line) into a User entity. The first element of the list indicates the user type ("Person" or "Duck").
      Specified by:
      extractEntity in class AbstractFileRepository<Long,User>
      Parameters:
      data - the list of string values representing the user's fields
      Returns:
      the User created from the list of strings
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the user type is invalid or unrecognized

    • createEntityAsString

      protected String createEntityAsString(User entity)
      Converts a User entity into a string suitable for storing in a file. Supports both Person and Duck types.
      Specified by:
      createEntityAsString in class AbstractFileRepository<Long,User>
      Parameters:
      entity - the User entity to convert
      Returns:
      the string representation of the user for file storage
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the user type is invalid or unrecognized
